mtp.connectedToast.title: Connected to {deviceName}

@mtp.connectedToast.title.description: Title of the toast shown when an Android/Kindle/camera device connects over USB.

placeholders.deviceName: the connected device's display name (for example Pixel 7), or a generic fallback

mtp.connectedToast.bodyMac: Cmdr paused the macOS camera daemon (ptpcamerad) to access this device. To use it in another app, disable MTP support in settings.

@mtp.connectedToast.bodyMac.description: Body of the device-connected toast on macOS, explaining that Cmdr paused the system camera daemon and how to release the device. ptpcamerad is a macOS system process name; keep it literal. MTP is a device-transfer protocol acronym; keep it.

mtp.connectedToast.bodyOther: To use this device in another app, disable MTP support in settings.

@mtp.connectedToast.bodyOther.description: Body of the device-connected toast on non-macOS platforms, telling the user how to release the device for other apps. MTP is a device-transfer protocol acronym; keep it.

@mtp.connectedToast.dontShowAgain.description: Checkbox label that suppresses the device-connected toast for future connections.

@mtp.connectedToast.disableMtp.description: Button that turns off MTP device support entirely. The ellipsis hints further confirmation/settings. MTP is a protocol acronym; keep it. Tight mini button.

@mtp.connectedToast.ok.description: Button that acknowledges and dismisses the device-connected toast. Tight mini button.

mtp.permissionDialog.title: Can''t access USB device

@mtp.permissionDialog.title.description: Title of the Linux dialog shown when Cmdr lacks permission to access a connected USB (MTP) device.

mtp.permissionDialog.description: Cmdr doesn''t have permission to access this device. Linux needs udev rules to grant MTP device access.

@mtp.permissionDialog.description.description: Main explanation in the Linux USB-permission dialog. udev is a Linux device-management term and MTP a protocol acronym; keep both literal.

mtp.permissionDialog.explanation: Run this command in your terminal to install the rules and reload them:

@mtp.permissionDialog.explanation.description: Instruction above the copyable terminal command in the Linux USB-permission dialog.

mtp.permissionDialog.helpText: After running the command, unplug and replug the device, then retry.

@mtp.permissionDialog.helpText.description: Follow-up instruction below the command in the Linux USB-permission dialog, telling the user to reconnect the device and retry.

@mtp.permissionDialog.close.description: Button that closes the Linux USB-permission dialog without retrying.

@mtp.permissionDialog.retry.description: Button that retries connecting to the USB device after the user installed the udev rules.

mtp.ptpcameradDialog.title: Can''t connect to MTP device

@mtp.ptpcameradDialog.title.description: Title of the macOS dialog shown when another process holds exclusive access to a connected MTP device. MTP is a protocol acronym; keep it.

mtp.ptpcameradDialog.inUseBy: The device is in use by <process>{process}</process>.

@mtp.ptpcameradDialog.inUseBy.description: Sentence naming which process currently holds the device. <process> wraps the process name and renders it emphasized (bold).

placeholders.process: the blocking process description (for example pid 45145, ptpcamerad)

mtp.ptpcameradDialog.inUseGeneric: Another process has exclusive access to the device.

@mtp.ptpcameradDialog.inUseGeneric.description: Fallback sentence shown when the blocking process can't be named, in the macOS MTP-conflict dialog.

mtp.ptpcameradDialog.explanation: On macOS, the system daemon ptpcamerad automatically claims Android devices. To work around this, run the following command in Terminal (keep it running while using Cmdr):

@mtp.ptpcameradDialog.explanation.description: Explanation in the macOS MTP-conflict dialog: the system camera daemon grabs Android devices, so a workaround command must run. <code> wraps the process name in monospace styling. ptpcamerad is a macOS process name and Terminal the macOS app; keep both literal.

mtp.ptpcameradDialog.helpText: This command continuously stops ptpcamerad while running. Press <key>Ctrl+C</key> in Terminal to stop it when done.

@mtp.ptpcameradDialog.helpText.description: Help text under the workaround command in the macOS MTP-conflict dialog, explaining how to stop it. <key> wraps the keyboard-key pill for Ctrl+C (a styled chip). ptpcamerad is a process name and Terminal the macOS app; keep both literal.

placeholders.key: wraps the inline Ctrl+C key pill; keep the wrapper, the key glyph is filled in by the app

@mtp.ptpcameradDialog.close.description: Button that closes the macOS MTP-conflict dialog without retrying.

@mtp.ptpcameradDialog.retry.description: Button that retries connecting to the MTP device after the user ran the workaround command.

@mtp.deviceFallbackName.description: Generic fallback name for a connected MTP device when its real display name isn't known yet. MTP is a protocol acronym; keep it.

mtp.error.exclusiveAccess: Another process has exclusive access{blocking, select, none {} other { (blocked by {blocking})}}

@mtp.error.exclusiveAccess.description: Error stored on an MTP device entry when another process holds it exclusively, optionally naming that process. Shown in the device's error state.

placeholders.blocking: the blocking process name, or the literal none when it isn't known

mtp.error.permissionDenied: USB permission denied. Install udev rules and reconnect

@mtp.error.permissionDenied.description: Error stored on an MTP device entry when USB access was denied (Linux). udev is a Linux device-management term; keep it literal.
